meta.title: Contributors | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: Recognition for the people who make ProxMenux possible — testers, reviewers and developers who shape each release.

header.title: Contributors
header.description: The ProxMenux project grows and thrives thanks to the contribution of its collaborators. This is the well-deserved recognition of their work — testing builds before release, catching regressions, reviewing PRs and shaping the direction of new features.

beyond.title: Beyond this list
beyond.body: Many more people contribute via bug reports, feature ideas in Discussions, beta testing and translations. The full list of code contributors lives on the <extlink>GitHub contributors graph</extlink>. The page below highlights the people who have been most actively involved in testing and reviewing ProxMenux releases.

testers.heading: Testers & reviewers
testers.intro: These were among the first to contribute to the project — testing, reporting issues and suggesting improvements that helped ProxMenux grow into what it is today.

contribute.heading: Want to contribute?
contribute.intro: Any contribution is welcome — code, testing, design, documentation, or just sharing your ideas:
contribute.tester: Tester — try the beta channel and report what works and what doesn't before stable releases.
contribute.developer: Developer — Bash + Next.js (TypeScript) + a bit of Python for the translation venv. PRs welcome on GitHub.
contribute.designer: Designer — UI improvements, icons, screenshots for documentation.
contribute.ideas: Ideas — open a thread in GitHub Discussions before opening a feature request issue.

coc.title: Read the Code of Conduct first
coc.body: Before submitting code or reporting issues, give the <coclink>Code of Conduct & Best Practices</coclink> a quick read. It covers expectations around code responsibility, security disclosure and PR scope.
